title: North Melbourne ruck Tristan Xerri in hot water for wiping blood on Bombers skipper Andy McGrath
plaintext:
North Melbourne big man Tristan Xerri will be scrutinised by the AFL after appearing to wipe his own blood on the face of Essendon captain Andy McGrath.

In what was a fiery contest, Xerri kicked a goal from directly in front before McGrath ran straight up to him to remonstrate.

Players from both sides came in to form a fracas, at the centre of which was McGrath and Xerri, who puts his hand to his nose and then wipes it on McGrath’s left cheek.

Xerri had been bleeding from his nose earlier in the second quarter.

“Oh, don’t do that, Tristan,” Garry Lyon said on Fox Sports.

“That’s what you don’t want to see in the game.”

The incident had shades of a clash between Collingwood champion Nathan Buckley and Geelong tagger Cameron Ling in 2002.

Buckley was suspended for one match for deliberately smearing blood from a cut eyebrow onto the guernsey of Ling in an attempt to force Ling from the field under the blood rule.

More to come ...
sentence: In what was a fiery contest, Xerri kicked a goal from directly in front before McGrath ran straight up to him to remonstrate.
context: In what was a fiery contest , Xerri kicked a goal from directly in front BMRSU to him to remonstrate .

title: Collard facing AFL investigation again for alleged slur
plaintext:
St Kilda player Lance Collard is under AFL investigation for an alleged homophobic slur, less than two years after serving a six-game ban for the same offence.

Late on Saturday, the Saints revealed the AFL integrity unit was looking into an incident involving their players during Friday's VFL game against Frankston.

The AFL website later reported that the player was Collard, who was banned in 2024 for slurs in the VFL.

Collard will also face scrutiny for high contact on a Frankston player, sparking an on-field brawl.

"St Kilda is aware of an alleged incident involving one of its players during last night's VFL match against Frankston," the club said.

"The club takes matters of this nature extremely seriously and is working closely with the AFL integrity unit as it conducts its investigation into the matter.

"While this process is underway, it would be inappropriate for the club to comment further."

Collard has played 15 AFL games for the Saints since his 2024 debut.
sentence: "St Kilda is aware of an alleged incident involving one of its players during last night's VFL match against Frankston," the club said.
context: " St Kilda is aware of an alleged incident involving one OIPDL night 's VFL match against Frankston , " the club said .

title: SA claim key wicket after more rain in Shield final
plaintext:
South Australia claimed the key wicket of Victorian young gun Oliver Peake after rain further disrupted the Sheffield Shield final.

Marcus Harris and Fergus O'Neill then survived to lunch on Saturday at Junction Oval, leaving the home side 5-150 in an evenly poised game.

SA made 198 in their first innings, just three short of a crucial batting bonus point. That means they must win the final to win back-to-back Shield titles for the first time.

If the final is drawn, Victoria will win the Shield as the top-ranked team.

Play was delayed for more than an hour on day three because of persistent rain, after interruptions on Thursday and Friday.

But the five-day game and the low scores so far mean a result is likely.

Bowling around the wicket and wide of the stumps, Nathan McAndrew dismissed Peake for 28 with a superb delivery that hit the top of off stump.

It left the Victorians in strife at 5-127, but Harris and O'Neill held firm as SA tried to build on their counter-punch.

Harris had made 40 from 121 balls and O'Neill was on 11.

Victoria scored 1-40 from 16.3 overs in Saturday's morning session.

The weather had cleared by early afternoon and conditions are expected to hold for the rest of the day.
sentence: It left the Victorians in strife at 5-127, but Harris and O'Neill held firm as SA tried to build on their counter-punch.
context: It LTVIS at 5 - 127 , but Harris and O'Neill held firm as SA tried to build on their counter - punch .

title: Freo coach flags Gather Round return and change of role for Hayden Young
plaintext:
Fremantle coach Justin Longmuir has forecast a Gather Round return and a possible change of role for injured midfielder Hayden Young.

Young picked up a hamstring injury in their first-round defeat to Geelong and despite playing out the game was ruled out of their last two games against Melbourne and Richmond.

The 24-year-old’s injury was to his left hamstring. He had surgery on his right hamstring last season and was sidelined for three months.

But the coach said Young’s current ailment would be short-term and there was evidence from both hamstring injuries that would help the club rebuild his body for the long-term.

“We don’t have long-term concerns,” Longmuir told ABC Radio prior to Saturday’s clash with Richmond at Optus Stadium.

“Both mechanisms the last two years when he has done the hamstring have been very similar.

“So there is some real evidence there for us to go to work behind the scenes to make sure we strengthen certain parts of his body.

“We expect him back in the next couple of weeks. It’s not a long-term injury by any means.

“We will just have to weigh it up as coaches in where we can get him into the team. And play him in areas of the ground that take away those risks.”

Young made his name at half-back before slotting into a midfield role last season.

The Dockers play Collingwood in Gather Round on Friday, April 10 which would be 27 days since Young damaged the hamstring in the round one clash against the Cats.
sentence:
And play him in areas of the ground that take away those risks.”

Young made his name at half-back before slotting into a midfield role last season.
context: And play him in areas of the ground that take away those risks . ” YMHNA half - back before slotting into a midfield role last season .

title: 'It's up to Leo': uncertainty over Messi's World Cup
plaintext:
Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni says it is up to Lionel Messi to decide whether he will play at this summer's World Cup.

Messi played his final competitive match for Argentina on home turf in September, but the man considered by many as the game's greatest ever player was coy over his participation in the tournament in America.

Uncertainty has swirled around Messi's potential involvement in a sixth World Cup ever since and ahead of friendly fixtures with Mauritania and Zambia, Scaloni admitted it was now completely up to 38-year-old.

"This is a question more for him but as far as I'm concerned, you already know what I think. I will do everything I can to make sure he is there," Scaloni told a press conference on Friday.

"I believe he has to be there, for the sake of football, but it's not me who decides. It's up to him, how he feels in his mind and his physical condition.

"It's difficult because it's not just Argentinians who want to see him, everyone wants to see him

"And then we have the dilemma as his coaches when he's there, if he's able to play the whole game or if he's not.

"I want him to be there, but he's the one who decides. I'm not going to stand here and say, 'let's see if I pick him or not'.

"He has earned the right to decide calmly. We are not in a rush. We know that whatever he decides will be the best thing for both him and the team. We hope he'll be there but I can't say much more."
sentence: Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni says it is up to Lionel Messi to decide whether he will play at this summer's World Cup.
context: Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni says it is up TLMTD whether he will play at this summer 's World Cup .

title: WATCH | Austria plans social media ban for children under 14
plaintext:
Austria’s conservative-led, three-party government plans to introduce a ban on social media use for children under 14, it said on Friday.

Cabinet members from the three ruling parties announced a deal between them on the principle of a ban, aimed at protecting children from “algorithms that are addictive” and content including sexual abuse, but could not say when it would begin, and they have yet to agree on how it will be implemented.

“We will decisively protect children and young people in future from the negative effects of social media,” said vice-chancellor Andreas Babler of the Social Democrats.

“We will no longer stand by and watch while these platforms make our children addicted and often also sick. The risks associated with this use were ignored for long enough, and it is time to act.”

Australia introduced a social media ban for under-16s in December, the first country to do so. Other countries are considering or moving towards similar bans. France’s lower house of parliament approved a ban for under-15s in January.

Draft legislation for Austria’s ban would be drawn up by the end of June, Babler and conservative junior minister for digitisation Alexander Proell said.

Babler also said the government would not list individual platforms the ban would apply to but would decide based on how addictive their algorithms are and whether they include content such as “sexualised violence”.

Reuters
sentence: Babler also said the government would not list individual platforms the ban would apply to but would decide based on how addictive their algorithms are and whether they include content such as “sexualised violence”.

title: Kremlin says Russia not seeking to undermine US business in CPC oil group
plaintext:
Russia is not seeking to undermine U.S. business interests in the Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC), Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Friday.

He was responding to a question about comments by several Russian experts, including on state TV, that Russia should ban oil supplies via the CPC from Kazakhstan in response to Washington's sanctions against Russia.

CPC, in which U.S. oil majors Chevron CVX.N and ExxonMobil XOM.N are large shareholders, exports its oil from a Russian terminal on the Black Sea.

"In addition to American partners, there are also our Kazakh partners involved, and Russia remains a reliable guarantor of global energy security, despite everything," Peskov told a daily conference call with reporters.

CPC, whose terminal is located near Russia's Black Sea port of Novorossiysk, was targeted by Ukrainian drones and has had to scale back its operations due to the attacks.

Peskov accused Ukraine of "energy blackmail" over the strikes, which he said were causing Russian, U.S. and Kazakh companies to suffer.

(Reporting by Dmitry Antonov. Writing by Vladimir Soldatkin. Editing by Mark Trevelyan and Mark Potter)
sentence: He was responding to a question about comments by several Russian experts, including on state TV, that Russia should ban oil supplies via the CPC from Kazakhstan in response to Washington's sanctions against Russia.
context: He was responding to a question about CBSRE , including on state TV , that Russia should ban oil supplies via the CPC from Kazakhstan in response to Washington 's sanctions against Russia .

title: Italy one win away from World Cup after blanking Northern Ireland
plaintext:
Italy finally cleared its first hurdle in the World Cup playoffs and has one more game to win to avoid missing the competition for the third consecutive time.

The four-time World Cup champions beat Northern Ireland 2-0 on Thursday night in Bergamo, getting a breakthrough goal from Sandro Tonali early in the second half before Tonali helped create the insurance marker for Moise Kean.

Tonali opened the scoring in the 56th minute with a half-volley that settled Italy’s nerves, and the Azzurri were largely in control from there.

In the 80th, Tonali sprayed a crossfield pass to Kean, who took a clean touch and slid a low finish home to double the lead.

“After taking the lead, we cleared our minds,” Italy coach Gennaro Gattuso said.

“We put in a good performance. This should be a confidence boost for Tuesday.

“We have to win. We have no other choice.”

Italy’s next step is a winner-take-all final on Tuesday against Bosnia and Herzegovina, which advanced by beating Wales 4-2 on penalties after a 1-1 draw in Cardiff.

A victory would send Italy to the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico, and finally end a qualifying drought that began with playoff failures in 2018 and 2022.

Field Level Media
sentence: Tonali opened the scoring in the 56th minute with a half-volley that settled Italy’s nerves, and the Azzurri were largely in control from there.
context: Tonali opened the scoring in the 56th minute with a half - volley that settled Italy ’s nerves , ATAWL in control from there .

title: Kimberley council to scrap COVID-19 policy
plaintext:
The Shire of Wyndham East Kimberley has formally rescinded its COVID-19 emergency response and recovery policy, marking a clear transition away from pandemic-specific governance measures.

The motion to rescind the policy was passed at the council meeting on March 24, after being first adopted in April 2020 during the height of the COVID-19 crisis.

The policy played a key role in guiding the shire’s response, providing direction on emergency management, adjustments to services, financial hardship support and advocacy efforts during an unprecedented global event.

However, council officers advised that the policy had served its purpose and was no longer required as a standalone document.

Wyndham East Kimberley shire president David Menzel said “Let’s hope we never see that (COVID-19) again.”

Council officers emphasised that removing the COVID-19 policy would not impact future emergency preparedness, with appropriate mechanisms remaining in place to respond effectively to any new crisis.
sentence: The policy played a key role in guiding the shire’s response, providing direction on emergency management, adjustments to services, financial hardship support and advocacy efforts during an unprecedented global event.
context: The policy played a KRIGT shire ’s response , providing direction on emergency management , adjustments to services , financial hardship support and advocacy efforts during an unprecedented global event .

title: Ryan Crowley: Former Fremantle Dockers player enters plea to allegations he attacked MAFS star Lauren Dunn
plaintext:
Former Fremantle Dockers player Ryan Crowley is set to go to trial, fighting a string of horror domestic violence accusations.

The 42-year-old allegedly repeatedly attacked his ex-girlfriend Lauren Dunn, a former Married at First Sight contestant turned DV advocate, between 2019 and 2025 in Cottesloe, Claremont and West Leederville.

A police prosecutor previously said one witness had described him assaulting her “to the point of almost killing her”.

But when Crowley fronted Perth Magistrates Court in person on Friday, he pleaded not guilty to 13 charges against him.

He had faced 16 but three were dropped.

A count of being armed or pretending to be armed in a way that may cause fear was heard for the first time, with the magistrate saying it involved a large kitchen knife.

The assaults allegedly included throttling and “using an aid to partially block her nose and mouth”, the magistrate said.

Crowley had his bail renewed and will next face the same court for a trial allocation date on April 14.

He spent time in custody last year but was released on bail after his lawyer argued he had struggled to manage his diabetes while on remand.

His bail conditions include not contacting or approaching within 200m of Ms Dunn, and not contacting any witnesses.

The court was previously told she had described their years-long relationship as “toxic” and “highly volatile”, and had fled to NSW.

Crowley played 188 games for Fremantle between 2005-2014.

1800 RESPECT (1800 737 732)
sentence: But when Crowley fronted Perth Magistrates Court in person on Friday, he pleaded not guilty to 13 charges against him.

title: Kubayi’s court outburst exposes ANC’s internal fractures
plaintext:
When the trough is so deep and richly filled with taxpayer money, who wouldn't do everything in their power to get close to it?

Mmamoloko Kubayi, the minister of justice and constitutional development, feels that she, with her BA degree and master’s degree in public and development management, is competent to dismiss as “irrational” a judgment of the high court.

Miffed that the court interdicted the ANC’s preparations for its Eastern Cape conference, Kubayi – convenor of party “deployees” in the province – claimed the court showed “a misunderstanding of ANC processes”.

Presumably, the three ANC members who launched the application are also ignorant… because they do not share your view?

The minister’s emotional outburst – accompanied by her determination that the conference would go ahead because so many people had already travelled to East London – points to something more, we would venture.

Why are so many of the ANC’s allegedly democratic electoral processes ending up in court?

Could it be because certain factions are trying to gerrymander the results to bolster their candidates for higher office?

The in-fighting does seem to show members are more concerned about winning positions – rather than the fate of ordinary South Africans.

Still, when the trough is so deep and richly filled with taxpayer money, who wouldn’t do everything in their power to get close to it?
sentence: Presumably, the three ANC members who launched the application are also ignorant… because they do not share your view?
context: Presumably , the three ANC members WLTAA also ignorant … because they do not share your view ?

title: Iran responded to US plan via intermediaries, awaiting reply: Tasnim
plaintext:
Iran has responded through unnamed intermediaries to a 15-point U.S. plan to end the war, news agency Tasnim reported on Thursday, and is now waiting for Washington's reply.

The exact contents of the U.S. plan, conveyed to Iran via Pakistan according to Pakistani officials, are not officially known.

"Iran's response to the 15 points proposed by the U.S. was officially sent last night through intermediaries, and Iran is awaiting the other side's response," Tasnim reported, citing an informed source.

title: More accolades roll in for promising batter Glide
plaintext:
Great Boulder teenager Daniel Glide has added to his Eastern Goldfields Cricket Association trophy by winning his club’s under-17 batting award.

Glide topped the EGCA averages with 329 runs at 47.00 — highlighted by his unbeaten 101 in round 12 against North Kalgoorlie, when the right-hander faced 45 deliveries and blasted 18 fours and a six.

title: Contract of acting chief executive at Shire of Coolgardie extended until end of August
plaintext:
The Shire of Coolgardie’s acting chief executive Sabine Taylor has had her contract extended by four months as the hunt for a permanent leader continues.

Councillors at Tuesday’s ordinary council meeting voted to extend Ms Taylor’s contract — which was due to expire at the end of this month — until August 31.

Shire president Paul Wilcox said the goal was to have the new chief executive “sitting in the chair” before the new acting chief executive contract expired.

“The decision . . . to extend the acting CEO was just in recognition of the fact that we wouldn’t have a permanent CEO in place before the current acting CEO contract was due to finish,” he said.

“Council resolved to offer a new contract to the current acting CEO that commences on April 1 and goes through till August 31, which gives us enough time to hopefully finalise that permanent recruitment process.”

Applications for the new permanent chief executive will be open until Friday afternoon, and Mr Wilcox said a shortlisting process will begin after that.

“We’re working with a recruiting consultant on gathering those applicants,” he said.

“We have a meeting scheduled to consider the shortlist of applicants, identify some suitable candidates, and proceed to interviews with a view to hopefully securing a permanent CEO.”

title: Court interdicts ANC Eastern Cape conference
plaintext:
Earlier this week, the ANC's secretary-general Fikile Mbalula, claimed that the ANC had followed all the necessary steps in organising the conference.

The Eastern Cape High Court in East London has halted the ANC’s provincial elective conference set for this weekend.

This comes after a group of disgruntled party members, including the provincial secretary, Lulama Ngcukayitobi, raised concerns about alleged membership rigging and irregular processes leading up to the conference.

They argued that the ANC would violate its own constitution if the conference proceeded with the alleged irregularities.

The judge postponed the hotly-contested conference and ordered that a new date be arranged.

“This honourable court issues an order directing the respondents to comply with the ANC’s government instruments, including but not limited to the conference guidelines and its constitution.

“Interdicting and restraining the respondents from holding the provincially elective conference of the Eastern Cape Province of the ANC scheduled to be held from 26 to 29 March 2026,” the court ruled.

The ANC were ordered to pay the costs of the application.
sentence: The Eastern Cape High Court in East London has halted the ANC’s provincial elective conference set for this weekend.
context: The Eastern Cape High Court in East London has halted the ANC ’s PECSF this weekend .

title: WATCH | Court grants interim interdict against ANC Eastern Cape conference
plaintext:
The high court in KuGompo City (formerly East London) has issued an interim interdict in favour of the disgruntled applicants who hauled the ANC to court to interdict the provincial ANC elective conference.

Acting judge Babalo Metu, pending the determination of the grievances raised by the applicants, directed the ANC, the ANC provincial executive committee, ANC secretary-general Fikile Mbalula, the provincial secretary and the respondents, to comply with the ANC’s governance instruments, including but not limited to the “Conference Guidelines” and its constitution.

Metu interdicted and restrained the respondents from holding the provincial elective conference scheduled to be held from March 26-29.

The judge further granted leave to parties to supplement these papers, to the extent necessary, for the determination of the outstanding reliefs sought.

The ANC, PEC and Mbalula are to pay the costs of the application.

Meanwhile, the respondents have filed papers appealing against the ruling.

Attorney Sinawo Makangela, for the applicants, said the conference cannot continue until the ANC finalises its internal processes. Makangela said the appeal will not suspend the operation of the interim order.

Daily Dispatch
sentence: The high court in KuGompo City (formerly East London) has issued an interim interdict in favour of the disgruntled applicants who hauled the ANC to court to interdict the provincial ANC elective conference.
context: The high court in KuGompo City ( formerly East London ) HIAII in favour of the disgruntled applicants who hauled the ANC to court to interdict the provincial ANC elective conference .

plaintext:
Two people were killed and three were wounded by falling debris after air defenses intercepted a ballistic missile on the outskirts of Abu Dhabi, the government media office said on Thursday.

title: Amorim had great ideas but they did not click at Man United, says Maguire
plaintext:
Manchester United defender Harry Maguire says former manager Ruben Amorim had strong ideas but they ultimately “didn’t work” at Old Trafford, further praising interim manager Michael Carrick for overseeing a smooth transition.

United have revived their season since Carrick took charge in January, rising into the Premier League’s top three after earning 23 points in 10 games, with only one defeat.

“I really like Ruben, he’s got great ideas. The ideas just didn’t work at Manchester United,” Maguire said about Amorim in an interview with The Guardian.

“It just didn’t click or work and us, as players, have got to take a lot of responsibility for that as well.”

Amorim was known for his back three system, but Maguire said he feels more comfortable in a back four.

“In the middle of a back three, it is more cautious, a sweeper-type role and not as much driving forward with the ball, which has been a big part of my game throughout my career.

“I feel like it has been a great transition. Credit to Michael and his staff for making it so smooth.”

Maguire was named last week in Thomas Tuchel’s 35-man England squad as they host Uruguay at Wembley Stadium on Friday, followed by a clash with Japan at the same venue four days later.
sentence: Credit to Michael and his staff for making it so smooth.
context: Credit TMAHS for making it so smooth .

title: Volkswagen owner Porsche SE reports earnings fall in 2025
plaintext:
Porsche SE, the holding company that controls Volkswagen, said on Wednesday it suffered a blow to its 2025 after-tax profit, weighed down by costs at the German vehicle group and luxury sports carmaker Porsche AG.

Porsche SE reported adjusted earnings after tax (ebitda) of €2.9bn (R57.05bn), down about 9% year on year. The group’s net debt fell slightly to €5.1bn (R100.33bn).

The holding company of Germany’s Porsche-Piech auto dynasty is Volkswagen’s largest investor, with 31.9% of shares and 53.3% of voting rights. It also owns 12.5% of Volkswagen subsidiary Porsche AG. The top shareholder’s profit was hit by billions of euros in costs after Porsche AG slammed the brakes on its electric vehicle rollout in response to weak demand, including in China, where sales have slumped.

Despite the drop in adjusted earnings, Porsche SE said its smaller investments generated €193m (R3.80bn) in profit last year, highlighting efforts to diversify its portfolio.

“Our unique network has become a key strategic asset that significantly contributes to the strong financial performance of our portfolio,” board chair Hans Dieter Poetsch said in a statement.

With the German automotive sector under pressure from steep tariffs, Chinese competition and a costly shift to EVs, Porsche SE has said it is exploring defence investments to diversify its portfolio.

Reuters
sentence: The group’s net debt fell slightly to €5.1bn (R100.33bn).
context: The group ’s NDFST € 5.1bn ( R100.33bn ) .

title: Man accused of stabbing boy with disability, 16, in park playground with butter knife granted bail in Supreme Court
plaintext:
A man accused of stabbing a boy with a disability in a South Brisbane park playground has been granted bail by the Supreme Court.

Sefik Ferizovic has been charged over the alleged stabbing of the boy, aged 16, in a Mansfield park last year.

Police allege the boy was sitting on a swing when Mr Ferizovic attacked him with a butter knife.

The child only suffered minor injuries.

Mr Ferizovic was charged with assault occasioning bodily harm while armed.

He is also charged with two other assault occasioning bodily harm offences in the same year.

The first offence relates to an incident on June 25 in Upper Mount Gravatt while another relates to an incident in Strathpine on August 25 – three days after the alleged altercation with the boy.

On Thursday in Brisbane Supreme Court, Supreme Court Justice Paul Smith granted bail to Mr Ferizovic.

He is next due to appear in Brisbane Magistrates Court on April 24, 2026.
sentence: Sefik Ferizovic has been charged over the alleged stabbing of the boy, aged 16, in a Mansfield park last year.
context: Sefik Ferizovic has been charged over TASOT boy , aged 16 , in a Mansfield park last year .

title: Immortalised Payne sends message with Flemington statue
plaintext:
Already immortalised as the first female jockey to win the Melbourne Cup, racing trailblazer Michelle Payne now has the statue to match.

Payne broke new ground in stunning fashion in 2015, steering 100-1 roughie Prince Of Penzance to one of the biggest upsets in the history of Australia's greatest race.

The 40-year-old hopes her new likeness at Flemington - crafted by artist Judith Leman and unveiled on Thursday - can inspire the masses.

"My life changed in 2015 when I won the Melbourne Cup and it has been the most amazing journey since," Payne said.

"To now have a statue here at Flemington is truly extraordinary. It feels very surreal.

"As a young girl, the Melbourne Cup was the race I dreamed of winning and knowing that dream came true fills me with immense pride.

"My hope is that everyone who views this statue feels encouraged to chase their dreams and ambitions, no matter how big they are."

Victoria Racing Club chairman Neil Wilson said Payne's legacy extends well beyond her accomplishments on the racetrack.

"Michelle is an inspiration to countless Australians and a powerful ambassador for female participation and the sport of horse racing," Wilson said.

"We are proud to recognise Michelle's achievements and her rightful place amongst some of the legends of our sport."

Payne's Cup-winning feat was matched last year when Jamie Melham became the second female jockey to win the race that stops a nation, aboard Half Yours.
sentence: "As a young girl, the Melbourne Cup was the race I dreamed of winning and knowing that dream came true fills me with immense pride.
context: " As a young girl , the Melbourne Cup was the race I DOWAK that dream came true fills me with immense pride .

title: Fruit squash drink recalled from shop shelves
plaintext:
The National Consumer Commission (NCC) has confirmed the recall of specific batches of the Slimsy pomegranate squash concentrate.

The recall applies to the 6% Slimsy Pomegranate Squash Concentrate One Litre with the best before date of October 14 2026. The products were made available for purchase between January 22 2026 and March 23 2026 and were distributed in Gauteng, Western Cape, Eastern Cape, Free State and KwaZulu-Natal.

The recall affects a total of 2,894 cases of the product, amounting to 17,364 units, that were made available to the market, according to the supplier, Dynamic Brands Manufacturing.

The defect relates to blown or swollen bottles of the product, which may rupture due to gas formation in the product and lead to physical injury. According to the supplier, ingestion of the product could potentially lead to foodborne illnesses.

Consumers who have purchased the affected products are urged not to consume these and to immediately return the items to the point of purchase for a full refund.

The NCC has called on retailers to immediately remove the affected products from shelves to prevent potential consumer harm.
sentence: The NCC has called on retailers to immediately remove the affected products from shelves to prevent potential consumer harm.
context: The NCC HCORT immediately remove the affected products from shelves to prevent potential consumer harm .

title: Western Power investigating cause of mass outage in Great Southern, with more than 25,000 customers affected
plaintext:
Half of the 25,000 customers affected by the mass power outage that hit the Great Southern region on Thursday morning, have had their power restored.

The outage spans almost 300km along the south coast, with residents in an area from Walpole in the west, Bremer Bay in the east and Kendenup in the north all affected.

Power was lost to the large swathes of the region at 8.51am, with approximately 25,529 customers losing power.

About 13,505 customers across the region had their power restored at 9.51am.

More than 12,020 residents are still without power as of 10am.

The estimated restoration time for majority of remaining customers is 12.30pm.

The City of Albany took to its Facebook page to urge patience to residents affected by outage.

“We understand how disruptive this is for our community, local businesses, and essential services,” the post read.

“In the meantime, please take care on the roads, check in on neighbours who may need support, and follow any advice from emergency services.

“We appreciate your patience and understanding while crews work to restore power as quickly and safely as possible.”

A Western Power spokesperson said they are investigating the cause of the outage and working to restore power as soon as possible.

More to come.
sentence: Power was lost to the large swathes of the region at 8.51am, with approximately 25,529 customers losing power.
context: Power was LTTLS of the region at 8.51am , with approximately 25,529 customers losing power .

title: European Council president reaffirms EU support for Lebanon in call with President Aoun
plaintext:
Lebanon’s President Joseph Aoun received a phone call from European Council President Antonio Costa, who briefed him on the latest developments related to the Israeli escalation across various areas of Lebanon and confirmed the European Union’s support for the negotiation initiative launched by the Lebanese president to put an end to the escalation in Lebanon.

Costa also affirmed the European Union’s support for Lebanon and its people during the difficult circumstances the country is facing, and the EU’s readiness to assist Lebanon in various sectors to help end the suffering of the Lebanese people.

President Aoun thanked Costa for the European Union’s position and for the humanitarian and medical aid sent to help Lebanese citizens who were forced to flee their homes in areas targeted by Israeli attacks, expressing hope that this assistance would increase given that the number of displaced people has exceeded one million.

President Aoun and Costa agreed to remain in contact to follow political and military developments and their repercussions.
sentence: President Aoun thanked Costa for the European Union’s position and for the humanitarian and medical aid sent to help Lebanese citizens who were forced to flee their homes in areas targeted by Israeli attacks, expressing hope that this assistance would increase given that the number of displaced people has exceeded one million.
context: President Aoun thanked Costa for the European Union ’s position and FTHAM aid sent to help Lebanese citizens who were forced to flee their homes in areas targeted by Israeli attacks , expressing hope that this assistance would increase given that the number of displaced people has exceeded one million .

title: Iran FM says we do not intend to negotiate
plaintext:
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Wednesday that Iran did not plan to negotiate with the United States and that Tehran intended to keep fighting, after US President Donald Trump said Washington had proposed a peace plan.

"At present, our policy is the continuation of resistance", Araghchi said on state TV, adding: "We do not intend to negotiate -- so far, no negotiations have taken place, and I believe our position is completely principled."

"Speaking of negotiations now is an admission of defeat," he said.
sentence: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Wednesday that Iran did not plan to negotiate with the United States and that Tehran intended to keep fighting, after US President Donald Trump said Washington had proposed a peace plan.
context: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi SOWTI did not plan to negotiate with the United States and that Tehran intended to keep fighting , after US President Donald Trump said Washington had proposed a peace plan .

title: Lions' blow with McCluggage return on ice
plaintext:
Hugh McCluggage's injured calf is healing slower than expected, delaying the Brisbane Lions star's return.

The All-Australian and newly-promoted co-captain was injured in the Opening Round loss to Western Bulldogs and missed the side's follow up defeat to Sydney.

With a bye last weekend, it was hoped the low-grade tear would heal in time for him to face St Kilda on Saturday.

But Brisbane coach Chris Fagan said the midfielder would miss the clash, and potentially their Easter Thursday blockbuster against Collingwood at the Gabba.

"He's not playing this weekend and whether he's right in five days, that's debatable," Fagan said.

"It's a low-grade calf injury that's not improving very quickly."

The Lions are already without suspended skipper Harris Andrews but will welcome back Zac Bailey and Darcy Gardiner, who both served one-match bans against the Swans.

Young forward Logan Morris (hand) is expected to be fit to play.

The two-time defending champions let a late lead slip against the Bulldogs before Sydney blew them away at the SCG.

"They know the urgency around trying to get that win on the board," Fagan said.

"It's not all doom and gloom."
sentence: But Brisbane coach Chris Fagan said the midfielder would miss the clash, and potentially their Easter Thursday blockbuster against Collingwood at the Gabba.
context: But Brisbane coach Chris Fagan said the midfielder would miss the clash , and PTETB against Collingwood at the Gabba .

title: New shark monitoring receiver to be installed in time for Margaret River Pro
plaintext:
A new shark monitoring receiver will be deployed at Margaret River Main Break off Surfers Point in time for the Margaret River Pro.

Three other receivers are already in place within the shire, including popular surfing spots at Lefthanders and Cowaramup Bay, with the fourth following consultation with the Shire of Augusta Margaret River, Surfing WA and Surf Life Saving WA.

Fisheries Minister Jackie Jarvis said the new receiver would further enhance safety for the local surfing community.

“Receivers are also in place at Cowaramup Bay, Lefthanders, Smiths Beach, Bunker Bay, Meelup, Busselton and Bunbury so locals and visitors can continue to enjoy our beautiful South West beaches with confidence,” she said.

The State Government’s Shark Monitoring Network provides near real-time alerts of tagged shark detections via the SharkSmart WA app and SharkSmart website.

The new receiver will be installed in time for the Margaret River Pro in April and will remain in place after the event.

There are 39 shark monitoring receivers along the Western Australian coastline, including 12 at popular South West beaches.

The Department of Primary Industries and Regional Development is also working with the Shire of Augusta Margaret River to link the Margaret River Main Break receiver to the existing Shark Warning System tower at Surfers Point to alert beach users of nearby shark activity.
sentence: The new receiver will be installed in time for the Margaret River Pro in April and will remain in place after the event.
context: The new receiver will BIITF the Margaret River Pro in April and will remain in place after the event .

title: Council supports call for safer pedestrian crossings in Dunsborough
plaintext:
A push for safer pedestrian crossings in Dunsborough was raised in council chambers this week, calling on Main Roads to act on “dangerous” conditions along Caves Road.

Deputy mayor Kate Cox brought forward the motion, pointing to rising concerns among residents about safely crossing the busy stretch between Dunsborough Lakes and the town centre.

“Recently, an online survey was undertaken in the Dunsborough community and they achieved about 370 signatures — a local resident put the petition up,” she said.

Cr Cox shared feedback from the petitioner, highlighting fears for children riding bikes to and from school amid heavy traffic and poor visibility.

“Dunsborough is only getting busier with the level of development and I think we need to proactively advocate to Main Roads on this issue,” she said.

Cr Peter Adams backed the motion, saying it was a “nightmare driving through Dunsborough, let alone being a pedestrian”.

The motion was carried with amendments by Cr Andrew Macnish to include any cost, logistical and timing implications along with the possible impact on CBD parking on the south side of Caves Road.
sentence: Cr Peter Adams backed the motion, saying it was a “nightmare driving through Dunsborough, let alone being a pedestrian”.
context: Cr Peter Adams backed the motion , saying it was a “ nightmare driving through Dunsborough , LABAP ” .

title: Sydney teacher charged with online grooming of 14yo girl
plaintext:
A school teacher has been charged with grooming after allegedly offering a 14-year-old girl money in exchange for sexually explicit material via social media.

Police arrested the 29-year-old educator in Sydney’s northern suburbs on Thursday morning.

Officers had received reports that a 14-year-old girl had been communicating with a man online who had allegedly encouraged her to send sexually explicit material in exchange for money.

The 29-year-old has been charged with using a carriage service to groom a person under the age of 16 for sex and to access child abuse material.

The man was refused bail and is due to appear at Burwood Local Court on Thursday.

The arrest was part of an ongoing police operation called Strike Force Trawler, which aims to crack down on online child exploitation.
sentence: The 29-year-old has been charged with using a carriage service to groom a person under the age of 16 for sex and to access child abuse material.
context: The 29 - year - old has been charged with using a carriage service to groom a person under the age of 16 for sex and TACAM .

title: Man charged with broad daylight murder of David Khou
plaintext:
Fifteen months after a Sydney man was gunned down in broad daylight near his suburban home, a man has been arrested and charged with his murder.

David Khou was allegedly shot multiple times in front of neighbours and children as he sat in his car out the front of his home in Canley Heights on December 29 2024.

Police said officers arrested a 31-year-old man at a Fairfield West home about 7am on Wednesday.

The man was charged with murder, damaging property using fire or explosives, knowingly or recklessly directing a criminal group to assist with a crime, failing to comply with handing over digital evidence and accompanying a driver of a stolen vehicle.

Police said emergency services found Mr Khou about 6.50pm on December 29, 2024.

“A 31-year-old man was treated at the scene by NSW Ambulance paramedics for gunshot wounds; however, he could not be revived. The man has since been identified as David Khou,” a police spokesman said.

“A short time later, emergency services were called to an abandoned car alight on Bathurst St in Wakeley.

“Two men – aged 33 and 22 – have since been charged and remain before the courts.”

The man arrested in the Fairfield raid is expected to appear at Fairfield Local Court on Thursday.

More to come
sentence: Police said officers arrested a 31-year-old man at a Fairfield West home about 7am on Wednesday.
context: Police said officers arrested a 31 - year - old MAAFW home about 7 am on Wednesday .

title: Bullseye! England find new weapon as World Cup looms
plaintext:
Luke Littler has been happy to teach the England soccer squad how to hit the target as he hosted a darts tournament for them in at St George's Park.

The teenage double world champion visited Thomas Tuchel's squad on Tuesday as they build up towards upcoming pre-World Cup friendlies with Uruguay and Japan.

Littler had dinner with the players before hosting the 'Three Lions Darts Championship' at their St George's Park base in Burton on Tuesday night.

Phil Foden even hit a 180 after a session throwing with Luke Littler.

Despite Phil Foden hitting a 180 in the final, Newcastle goalkeeper Aaron Ramsdale became camp champion to leave Littler impressed.

The 19-year-old, who travels to Berlin this week for the latest round of Premier League action, said: "It was a dream as a football fan to come here to see the facilities, meet all the players and sit down and have dinner and have a laugh with them.

"We played a bit of darts – as they were warming up, Thomas Tuchel told me to teach these boys a lesson! There's some good potential there in Phil Foden and Aaron Ramsdale.

"James Trafford had a little bit as well. The others not so much! I wished the boys luck this summer – let's hope they can bring it home."

Littler was presented with a signed shirt, with Jude Bellingham also giving him a signed jersey with the message "Keep smashing it" on the back.
sentence: I wished the boys luck this summer – let's hope they can bring it home.
context: I wished the boys luck this summer – let 's hope TCBIH .

title: White House warns Iran against balking at deal: Trump ready to 'unleash hell'
plaintext:
The White House issued a stark warning to Iran on Tuesday, saying President Donald Trump is prepared to "unleash hell" if Tehran refuses to step back from its nuclear ambitions and its ongoing threats against the United States and its allies.

"President Trump does not bluff, and he is prepared to unleash hell," White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said at the beginning of Wednesday's White House press briefing. "Iran should not miscalculate again. Their last miscalculation cost them their senior leadership, their navy, their air force and their air defense system.

"Any violence beyond this point will be because the Iranian regime refused to understand. They have already been defeated and refused to come to a deal."

Leavitt said ongoing U.S. military operations, dubbed Operation Epic Fury, have severely weakened Iran's offensive capabilities. She claimed more than 9,000 targets have been struck, Iranian missile and drone attacks have fallen by about 90%, and much of Iran’s navy and air defense network has been destroyed.

Leavitt said the campaign is aimed at crippling Iran’s military infrastructure and securing the Strait of Hormuz, while also pressuring Tehran to return to talks. She added that Trump has temporarily delayed planned strikes on some energy targets to allow space for negotiations.

Despite saying the president prefers peace, Leavitt warned that any further Iranian resistance would bring even heavier U.S. retaliation.
sentence: "Any violence beyond this point will be because the Iranian regime refused to understand.
context: " Any violence beyond TPWBB the Iranian regime refused to understand .

title: US 15-point plan conveyed to Iran via Pakistan: Officials to AFP
plaintext:
Proposals from the United States to end the war in Iran have been sent to Tehran through Pakistani intermediaries, two senior officials in Islamabad told AFP on Wednesday.

Both confirmed that the 15-point plan to stop the fighting, which has spread across the Gulf and Middle East, had been "conveyed to Iran via Pakistan."

Pakistan is being touted as a possible mediator given its longstanding ties with both neighboring Iran and the United States, as well as close contacts in the region.
sentence: Proposals from the United States to end the war in Iran have been sent to Tehran through Pakistani intermediaries, two senior officials in Islamabad told AFP on Wednesday.
context: Proposals from the United States to end the war in Iran have been sent to Tehran through Pakistani intermediaries , TSOII told AFP on Wednesday .

title: LISTEN: ‘I was rejected my whole life’: William Last KRM on building success after failure
plaintext:
William made an aappearance on 'My Top 10 with Tbose' on Kaya FM.

Comedian William Last KRM recently opened up about his rise in the entertainment industry during his appearance on My Top 10 with Tbose on Kaya FM.

He said his journey into content creation began in 2017 after failing school.

With no clear plan he started creating videos to pass time, working alone with a camera.

Rejection and challenges

He said the rejection was a defining part of his early life and career.

“I was rejected my whole life, jobs-wise and life in general, I’ve been rejected by people, you know, certain stuff,” he said.

“Let’s say I was in talks with a certain company and then it doesn’t go through. I’m used to disappointment since I was a kid,” he added.

Last said these experiences motivated him to work harder.

He said as his profile grew, he encountered challenges on the business side of the industry.

He said an early management decision to set high fees resulted in missed opportunities while he was still building an audience.

“I was not even half a million followers then,” he said. “This guy was charging a whole estate… when you’re new, you need to build.”

You can listen to the full interview below:
sentence: He said his journey into content creation began in 2017 after failing school.
context: He said HJICC began in 2017 after failing school .

title: Tiffany Meek enters not guilty plea for son’s murder, remains in custody
plaintext:
A 31-year-old woman accused of killing her son has pleaded not guilty in the Johannesburg high court sitting in Palm Ridge.

Tiffany Meek entered her plea on Wednesday in connection with the death of her 11-year-old son, Jayden-Lee. She faces charges of murder, crimen injuria, defeating the ends of justice and attempting to obstruct justice.

The state withdrew a charge of child abuse, citing insufficient evidence.

The matter was postponed to April 22 for the continuation of proceedings.

Jayden-Lee’s body was found on May 14 2025, on a staircase at the Swazi’s Place complex in Fleurhof, southwest of Johannesburg. He had been reported missing the previous day after being dropped off by scholar transport outside the complex.

According to the state, the child had returned home from school and was allegedly killed inside the house. Prosecutors claim he was struck with a blunt object, with blood later found on his bed and school uniform. It is further alleged that the scene was staged to mislead investigators.

The court ordered that Meek remain in custody.
sentence: Tiffany Meek entered her plea on Wednesday in connection with the death of her 11-year-old son, Jayden-Lee.
context: Tiffany Meek entered her plea on WICWT death of her 11 - year - old son , Jayden - Lee .

title: Madlanga witness among 12 arrested officers for Matlala’s SAPS tender
plaintext:
One of the 12 senior police officials who were arrested on Tuesday night in connection with the R360m SAPS tender awarded to Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala was a witness at the Madlanga commission.

Most of the arrested SAPS officials were involved in the bid evaluation committee that implemented the tender and gave it to Matlala’s company. They are expected to appear alongside the company’s director at the Pretoria magistrate’s court on Wednesday.

The arrests include:

One of the arrested police officers has already appeared before the Madlanga commission, where she had to explain her relationship with Matlala.

They have been charged with fraud, money laundering and misuse of public funds.

National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) spokesperson Kaizer Kganyago confirmed that the suspects will make their first appearance on Wednesday.

Medicare24, Matlala’s company, had secured a R350m contract to conduct the health assessments of new recruits in June 2024, but that was cancelled in May 2025 after an investigation showed that the tender was irregularly awarded.

Sowetan
sentence: One of the 12 senior police officials who were arrested on Tuesday night in connection with the R360m SAPS tender awarded to Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala was a witness at the Madlanga commission.

title: Andile Mxakaza on exploring polygamy in series ‘Isithembu sika Msongelwa’
plaintext:
Andile Mxakaza has opened up about his role as Msongelwa Shange on hit Mzansi Magic drama series Isithembu sika Msongelwa as it makes its return on April 6.

In the series that gives viewers an unflinching look at polygamy, the formidable trio of wives is portrayed by Nompumelelo Vilakazi (Senzeni), Phumzile Mlangeni (Mam’Zobe) and Thandeka Shangase (Phathekile). The show focuses on how they handle Nosipho Pehlo, who plays the role of Asanda stepping in to disrupt the Msongelwa household.

Andile describes the role as both challenging and deeply personal, highlighting how the series portrays polygamy not just as a lifestyle, but as a complex emotional and spiritual journey.

“The show reflects both the beauty and the difficulties of polygamy. It’s a constant reminder that if you’re not fully committed to that life, the consequences can be overwhelming,” he said.

“Beyond the drama, the series carries a strong message about accountability, respect and the importance of communication in relationships. It explores the consequences of choice, the value of emotional and spiritual alignment and the evolving roles of men and women within traditional structures.”
sentence: In the series that gives viewers an unflinching look at polygamy, the formidable trio of wives is portrayed by Nompumelelo Vilakazi (Senzeni), Phumzile Mlangeni (Mam’Zobe) and Thandeka Shangase (Phathekile).
context: In the series that GVAUL at polygamy , the formidable trio of wives is portrayed by Nompumelelo Vilakazi ( Senzeni ) , Phumzile Mlangeni ( Mam’Zobe ) and Thandeka Shangase ( Phathekile ) .

title: VW mulls shift from cars to missile defence at Osnabrueck plant
plaintext:
Volkswagen is in talks with Israel’s Rafael Advanced Defence Systems over a deal that would shift production at the carmaker’s Osnabrueck plant from cars to missile defence, the Financial Times reported, citing people familiar with the plan.

The companies aim to convert the German factory to make components for the Israeli state-owned group’s Iron Dome air defence system, the report said.

Volkswagen said it continued to explore solutions for the factory, adding that, while it was in discussions with market participants, it ruled out the production of weapons.

Germany’s defence ministry declined to comment.

Volkswagen wants to sell or reconfigure the site, which employs about 2,300 people, after the end of T-Roc Cabriolet production in 2027 as part of a wider revamp.

Talks with Rheinmetall about a sale stalled late last year, but Volkswagen CEO Oliver Blume said earlier this month that the company remained in talks with defence companies over a solution for Osnabrueck.

Reuters
sentence: The companies aim to convert the German factory to make components for the Israeli state-owned group’s Iron Dome air defence system, the report said.
context: The companies aim to convert the GFTMC for the Israeli state - owned group ’s Iron Dome air defence system , the report said .

title: Whale shark swim added to luxury camp stay
plaintext:
Sal Salis Ningaloo Reef is an eco-camp in the dunes on the North West Cape, near Yardie Creek and just a sandy-footed few steps to the ocean and Ningaloo Reef.

And until March 31, 2026, it is offering each traveller a free whale shark swim when they stay for a minimum of three nights.

As an example, in October the price is from $1980 per night, per couple. There is a three-night minimum.

Sal Salis has 15 wilderness tents, and a dining area and bar called The Lodge, which is the heart of the place.

The tents have a bed of handcrafted jarrah, fine linen, an ensuite bathroom and a daily cleaning service.

There’s a private deck and hammock — and the coral reef is just metres away.

A spokesperson says: “Back at camp, spend your days unwinding in barefoot luxury.

“Stay in eco-luxury steps away from UNESCO World Heritage-listed Ningaloo Reef, with time spent uncovering the underwater world, relaxing in your hammock, joining guided experiences along the coastline, enjoying destination dining, and completely slowing down to nature’s pace.”

For the included whale shark swim, see salsalis.com.au/stay-swim.
sentence: As an example, in October the price is from $1980 per night, per couple.
context: As an example , IOTPI from $ 1980 per night , per couple .

title: Two more arrested over abducted and murdered grandad
plaintext:
A man and a woman have been arrested in an early morning raid over the alleged mistaken kidnapping and killing of an elderly grandfather.

The body of widower Chris Baghsarian was found after he was allegedly abducted from his Sydney home before dawn on February 13 in a case of mistaken identity.

The pair, both 23, were taken to separate police stations for questioning from their residence in Seven Hills, in western Sydney, on Wednesday morning.

Their arrests come nearly a month after two other men - Gerard Anthony Andrews, 29, and Daniel Stevens, 24 - were charged for their role in the incident.

Investigators believe Mr Baghsarian, 85, was killed at a makeshift stronghold in Dural, in Sydney's outer north, before his body was dumped on the night of February 14 in Pitt Town, a 30-minute drive away.

His remains were located 10 days later near a golf club in Pitt Town.

Authorities believe the intended target for the abduction was a relative of Sydney businessman Dimitri Stepanyan, who lived on the same street as Mr Baghsarian.

Police suspected the intended target was to be kidnapped in hope of a ransom fee being paid and confirmed reports the figure demanded was $50 million.

Mr Baghsarian's family described the harrowing ordeal of the kidnapping as a nightmare, remembering him as a devoted father, brother, uncle and grandfather "who would never hurt a fly".
sentence: Police suspected the intended target was to be kidnapped in hope of a ransom fee being paid and confirmed reports the figure demanded was $50 million.
context: Police suspected the intended target was to be kidnapped in hope of a ransom fee being paid ACRTF demanded was $ 50 million .

title: Undefeated Comets on another level in Manjimup senior women A grand final
plaintext:
Comets capped off their undefeated season with a four-point grand final victory over the Titans in Friday night’s Manjimup Amateur Basketball Association senior women A.

Led by competition best and fairest Courtney Webb, the Comets were able to lead from start to finish but not without a fight from leading scorer and hot hand Maddi Gunson from Titans.

Comets kept their opponents at arm’s length all evening but Gunson ensured the margin was close for Titans, eventually falling short 32-28 and Comets ending their season undefeated.

Webb was named grand final MVP for Comets with eight points, helped by Taizley Krispyn (7) and Brianne Gluck (6), while Gunson finished with 15 for Titans.

Astrix stormed through the senior women B from third place to a dominant 22-point grand final win over Senators.

The margin was extended to five points at the start of the second half, but a commanding nine-point run extended the margin for Astrix and they never looked back winning 41-19.

Grand final MVP was awarded to Charlotte McGuire, who scored 15 points for Astrix, while Angela Fontanini supported her with 10.

Kim Reeve led the way for Senators with eight joined by Laura Bachos who scored six.

Best and fairest for the division was awarded to Lyndsay Bogoias.
sentence: Comets kept their opponents at arm’s length all evening but Gunson ensured the margin was close for Titans, eventually falling short 32-28 and Comets ending their season undefeated.
context: Comets kept their opponents at arm ’s length all evening BGETM was close for Titans , eventually falling short 32 - 28 and Comets ending their season undefeated .

title: Pemberton Hotel to celebrate a century next month
plaintext:
The Pemberton Hotel will commemorate 100 years in the town next month with a three-day centenary celebration.

The milestone marks a century since the hotel was reconstructed, after the transfer of its licence from Greenbushes earlier that year.

Originally built in the early 1900s as the Courthouse Hotel in Greenbushes, the hotel consisted of 29 bedrooms, a fine-dining room, billiard room, saloon bar, two upstairs parlours and two downstairs parlours.

In April 1926, the hotel was moved, rebuilt and opened in Pemberton.

Over the past century, the Pemberton Hotel has operated as a licensed venue and accommodation provider, serving locals and visitors alike.

Pemberton hotel owner Joanne Drake said the weekend would be a once-in-a-century celebration.

“It will bring together locals, visitors and friends for an unforgettable weekend,” they said.

“From stories and song to food and firelight, it’s our chance to honour the past and raise a glass to the next 100 years.”

The centenary festival will be held across the week of April 24-26.

Organisers have promised the event will include a program of activities highlighting the hotel’s history, alongside entertainment and food.

Further details of the program are set to be released in the coming weeks.
sentence:
“From stories and song to food and firelight, it’s our chance to honour the past and raise a glass to the next 100 years.”

The centenary festival will be held across the week of April 24-26.
context: “ FSAST food and firelight , it ’s our chance to honour the past and raise a glass to the next 100 years . ” The centenary festival will be held across the week of April 24 - 26 .

title: Rhys Hugh Walker MacFarlane: ‘Stalker’ told to speak to lawyer before sentencing
plaintext:
A Kalgoorlie-Boulder man who admitted to stalking another person was encouraged by a local magistrate to seek legal advice before being sentenced.

Rhys Hugh Walker MacFarlane, 27, represented himself in Kalgoorlie Magistrates Court on Monday and pleaded guilty to the March 6 and 7 charge of pursuing another to intimidate, but no details were read to the court.

MacFarlane said he had spoken to a lawyer and wished to “be honest”, plead guilty and apply for a spent conviction, noting he had character references and counselling documents ready to provide to the court.

Prosecuting Const. Kim Sullivan said the spent conviction would be opposed by police.

Magistrate William Yoo then asked MacFarlane if he would like to speak to a lawyer as it could be “difficult to persuade” him in his favour.

Mr Yoo suggested MacFarlane could indicate a guilty plea to preserve his right to a discounted sentence, and seek legal advice ahead of a future sentencing date.

“It’s your life, you have certain rights, what do you want to do?” he asked.

MacFarlane confirmed he would like to plead guilty, and Mr Yoo adjourned the sentencing for two months to allow him time to seek legal representation and submit his documents to the court for consideration.

He will be sentenced in May.
sentence: A Kalgoorlie-Boulder man who admitted to stalking another person was encouraged by a local magistrate to seek legal advice before being sentenced.
context: A Kalgoorlie - Boulder man who admitted to stalking another person was encouraged by ALMTS legal advice before being sentenced .

title: Modi, Trump discuss importance of keeping Strait of Hormuz open
plaintext:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump spoke by phone on Tuesday and discussed the situation in the Middle East, including the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open, said US envoy to India Sergio Gor and Modi in separate posts on X.

This was the first call between the two leaders since the US and Israel launched co-ordinated attacks against Iran on February 28.

“India supports de-escalation and restoration of peace at the earliest. Ensuring that the Strait of Hormuz remains open, secure and accessible is essential for the whole world,” Modi said in his post.

The conflict has disrupted sectors from air travel to shipping and fuel supplies, including by the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which serves as a conduit for 40% of India’s crude oil imports.

Modi told parliament on Monday that the crisis had triggered unprecedented challenges for India and added that the fundamentals of the Indian economy were strong and the country had adequate availability of petroleum, fertilisers and coal to weather trade and energy disruptions.

A White House official confirmed the call between Trump and Modi but did not comment on what was discussed.

Reuters
sentence: A White House official confirmed the call between Trump and Modi but did not comment on what was discussed.
context: A White House OCTCB Trump and Modi but did not comment on what was discussed .

title: Russia says it would be very concerned by Iran war spillover into Caspian Sea
plaintext:
Russia would view any spillover of the Iran conflict into the Caspian Sea "extremely negatively", Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Tuesday.

Israel said last week its military had carried out strikes on the Iranian navy in the Caspian, hitting dozens of targets including missile boats. A military spokesperson said Israel had managed to take out Iran's naval capabilities there.

Russia and Iran, which signed a strategic partnership treaty last year, both border the Caspian Sea.

On the state of negotiations between Washington and Tehran, Peskov said the Kremlin was observing contradictory statements from both parties.

(Reporting by Dmitry Antonov, writing by Anna Peverieri; Editing by Mark Trevelyan)
sentence: On the state of negotiations between Washington and Tehran, Peskov said the Kremlin was observing contradictory statements from both parties.
context: On TSONB Washington and Tehran , Peskov said the Kremlin was observing contradictory statements from both parties .

title: Captivate Capel has been cancelled after a grim weather warning
plaintext:
A beloved South West festival has been cancelled, with ‘Mother Nature’ cited as the official party pooper.

Captivate Capel was set to take place on Saturday March 28, however the forecast fierce winds and heavy rain has prevented the festivities from going ahead.

The decision has come after the Bureau of Meteorology forecast up to 25mm of rain with a top of 22C on Saturday, with very little reprieve on Sunday.

Ex-Tropical cyclone Narelle has already whipped torrential rain through the State’s north while the poor weather is set to head south in the coming days, with Perth looking to cop a very wet weekend.

The Shire of Capel announced the cancellation in a Facebook post on Tuesday afternoon.

“This decision wasn’t made lightly, but keeping our community, vendors, performers and stallholders safe comes first,” the post said.

“A massive thank you to everyone who was set to be part of this year’s event.

“From our incredible vendors and entertainers to the behind-the-scenes legends, we appreciate you more than a sunny forecast.”
sentence: Captivate Capel was set to take place on Saturday March 28, however the forecast fierce winds and heavy rain has prevented the festivities from going ahead.
context: Captivate Capel was set to take place on Saturday March 28 , HTFFW and heavy rain has prevented the festivities from going ahead .

title: ‘This chapter has been special’: Kea Zawadi bids Algoa FM farewell
plaintext:
Kea Zawadi has announced she is leaving Algoa FM after three years.

During her time at the station, the media personality and actress hosted the Algoa FM Top 30 and week nights from 7pm to 10pm.

Reflecting on her journey on the show, Kea said she was excited about the new chapter in her life.

“This chapter has been so special to me. From the first show to now, it’s been a journey of growth, learning and connection. I’m incredibly grateful to everyone who listened, sent messages and allowed me to be part of their daily lives,” she said in a statement shared on her Instagram timeline.

“This is not a goodbye, just the beginning of a new chapter.”

Kea will do her final show on Algoa FM on March 24.

Another media personality who marked three years at Algoa FM this year is Carly Fields. She took to her timeline to reflect on her journey.

“Three years and still smiling. I love what I do and grateful I get to do what I love. Thank you, Algoa FM and Alfie Jay, for giving me this opportunity to live my dream every day,” she said.
sentence: I get to do what I love.
context: IGTDW I love .

title: ABC’s 7.30  to not air, re-runs planned for Wednesday’s broadcast as thousands  staff take part in 24-hour strike
plaintext:
The ABC will dump its scheduled broadcast of its flagship current affairs program on Wednesday night, as thousands of the national broadcast’s workers walk off the job amid an ongoing pay dispute.

The 24-hour strike, beginning at 11am on Wednesday, is expected result in regularly-scheduled programs across the ABC’s main television channel and dedicated news channel not airing at all.

Programming across the ABC’s radio network will also be heavily impacted.

According to the latest TV guides, the network’s flagship current affairs show 7.30 has been axed in favour of an episode of Hard Quiz Battle of the Networks.

An episode of Australian Story will run in place of the broadcaster’s regular 7pm news bulletin.

A pre-recorded new episode of The Weekly With Charlie Pickering will run in its usual 8.30pm slot, as the program is normally shot on Tuesdays.

The ABC’s radio broadcasting schedule has been thrown into chaos.

The Sydney Morning Herald reports the network’s audio director Ben Latimer has told staff they will primarily be playing re-runs of existing content and BBC broadcasts throughout the strike period.

Radio National’s Late Night Live will also be running a rerun on Wednesday.

ABC hosts began informing listeners of the strike on Tuesday.

The Community and Public Sector Union said after months of negotiations ABC staff had “strongly rejected” the latest pay offer.

The last time 24 hour strike at the ABC was in 2006.
sentence: The ABC’s radio broadcasting schedule has been thrown into chaos.
context: The ABC ’s radio BSHBT into chaos .

title: Nation-leading laws pump brakes on illegal e-bikes
plaintext:
TOUGH NEW LAWS UNDER QUEENSLAND GOVERNMENT E-BIKES CRACKDOWN

What is Queensland proposing?

* Riders must be at least 16 years old to use e-bikes and e-scooters.

* Users will be required to hold at least a Queensland learner licence

* Strict 10 km/h speed limits are proposed for some pathways.

* Recommendations include restricting the importation and sale of non-compliant, high-power devices.

* Parents and guardians will be held legally responsible for fines incurred by children under 16 who are caught riding e-bikes or e-scooters.

* Queensland Police will be granted new powers to seize and potentially destroy illegal or modified e-mobility devices on a first offence.

* Officers will be authorised to conduct random breath tests on e-bike and e-scooter riders to crack down on drink-riding.

* Stricter oversight and fines will be introduced for retailers who sell modification kits designed to increase power or speed.

* Devices capable of exceeding 25km/h or that have a motor larger than 250W will be reclassified as motorcycles or mopeds and will need to be registered and insured.

* The new laws are expected to take effect from July 1, 2026, following a period of public education.
sentence:
Devices capable of exceeding 25km/h or that have a motor larger than 250W will be reclassified as motorcycles or mopeds and will need to be registered and insured.

*
context: Devices capable of exceeding 25 km/h or that have a motor larger than 250W will be reclassified AMOMA will need to be registered and insured . *

title: Man charged after fuel stolen, attendant run over
plaintext:
An accused petrol thief has run over a service station attendant, as fuel costs soar due to war in the Middle East.

A 39-year-old man has been charged after the attendant was taken to hospital with serious injuries following the alarming incident at a Western Australian petrol station.

The man is accused of filling up his vehicle at an Armadale station in southeast Perth about 1pm on Monday before attempting to leave without paying.

A male attendant was struck and injured when he attempted to stop the sedan, police said.

"The attendant sustained serious facial injuries and was conveyed by ambulance to hospital for medical treatment," police said in a statement.

Detectives tracked down the alleged fuel thief hours later, arresting him about 11.40pm on Monday.

The man from Armadale has been charged with a string of offences including stealing and dangerous driving occasioning grievous bodily harm.

He is due to appear at Armadale Magistrates Court on Tuesday.

The incident occurred as fuel prices surge, with shortages hitting petrol stations nationwide amid the Middle East conflict.
sentence: Detectives tracked down the alleged fuel thief hours later, arresting him about 11.40pm on Monday.
context: Detectives tracked DTAFT hours later , arresting him about 11.40pm on Monday .

title: Identity of man accused of encouraging ex-colleagues to die by suicide and sending their details to funeral homes revealed
plaintext:
The identity of a man accused of giving the details of his former colleagues to funeral homes after encouraging them to die by suicide has been revealed.

Adrian Bruno, 38, is accused of sending “graphic and confronting” emails to some of his former female colleagues.

Some of the threatening emails allegedly encouraged the women to die by suicide, police allege.

Mr Bruno is further accused of giving his alleged victims’ contact details to funeral homes, which then contacted the women to make arrangements for their funeral services.

The 38-year-old fronted Burwood Local Court for the first time on Tuesday morning.

Clutching a bible, Mr Bruno asked for an adjournment to seek legal advice.

His request was granted, with his matter to return to court on April 14 when he is due to enter pleas.

“I can leave? I can go home?” Mr Bruno asked the registrar.

Mr Bruno declined to comment on the case to journalists outside court, telling them “I’m seeking legal advice”.

He was arrested on March 16 and charged with 17 counts of using a carriage service to menace/harass/offend, use carriage service with intent for another to commit suicide, publish etc false misleading material to obtain advantage and destroy or damage property.

Mr Bruno will remain on bail ahead of his next court appearance, with interim apprehended violence orders to continue until that date.
sentence: Some of the threatening emails allegedly encouraged the women to die by suicide, police allege.
context: Some of the threatening emails allegedly encouraged TWTDB suicide , police allege .

title: Milan Murdock: West Coast Eagles bolter set to be sidelined with hamstring setback
plaintext:
The Milan Murdock story has been a stunning one - but it’s momentarily on pause as the mature-age bolter succumbs to an injury.

Seven’s Mitch Cleary is reporting the West Coast forward has suffered a hamstring strain that is set to sideline the 25-year-old.

Murdock reportedly played out Sunday’s win over North Melbourne with a hamstring issue, with subsequent scans revealing a strain.

The club is set to release its injury report later on Tuesday.

Murdock has made a remarkable start to his AFL career after only joining the Eagles a fortnight before the season. He ranks among the league’s top 10 players for average contested possessions, tackles and ground-ball gets – despite not having attended a centre ball-up this season.

The East Fremantle product’s expected absence means West Coast will be forced into at least two changes against Port Adelaide on Sunday, with key defender Harry Edwards to miss through concussion.

More to come
sentence: Murdock reportedly played out Sunday’s win over North Melbourne with a hamstring issue, with subsequent scans revealing a strain.
context: Murdock reportedly played out Sunday ’s win over North Melbourne with a hamstring issue , WSSRA strain .

title: Arzi municipality urges residents to seek safety after evacuation calls
plaintext:
Residents of the southern town of Arzi have received calls urging them to evacuate, prompting a response from local authorities.

In a statement, the municipality said a number of residents reported receiving calls instructing them to leave the town. It urged residents to move to safer areas, while advising those who choose to remain to avoid gathering and stay dispersed in separate locations.
sentence: Residents of the southern town of Arzi have received calls urging them to evacuate, prompting a response from local authorities.
context: Residents of the southern town of Arzi have received calls urging them to evacuate , PARFL authorities .

title: Connor Wright claims a clean sweep at the Mountain Bike National Championships
plaintext:
Burekup’s Connor Wright claimed a clean sweep at the 2026 Mountain Bike National Championships in Mt Buller, Victoria over the weekend.

Wright continues to show he’s the best thing on the dirt and the road in Australian cycling, winning races and taking home gold in both disciplines in the last few months.

The first event of the weekend was the team relay in which Wright helped his team East West Spectacular to victory by more than seven minutes, with teammates Jack Ward, Sarah Tucknott and Jon Odam.

Wright defended his national title in the short-course XCC junior men’s division, leading from start to finish, with the final margin 37 seconds.

He was also dominant in the junior men’s cross-country title, winning by a mammoth 4min. 8sec. to bring home his third and final gold of the weekend.
sentence: Wright continues to show he’s the best thing on the dirt and the road in Australian cycling, winning races and taking home gold in both disciplines in the last few months.
context: Wright continues to show he ’s the best thing on the dirt and TRIAC , winning races and taking home gold in both disciplines in the last few months .

title: Colombian military plane with 110 soldiers crashes
plaintext:
Colombia's defence ministry says a Lockheed Martin Hercules C-130 plane was involved in an accident in the south of the country, with local outlet BluRadio reporting that 110 soldiers were travelling on board.

Defence Minister Pedro Sanchez said the accident happened as the plane was taking off from Puerto Leguizamo, deep in Colombia's southern Amazon region on the border with Peru, as it transported troops from the armed forces.

"The exact number of victims and the causes of the crash have not yet been determined," he said.

BluRadio cited authorities as saying 110 soldiers were on board, and that the crash took place just 3km from an urban centre.

US defence company Lockheed Martin did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

At the end of February, another Hercules C-130 belonging to the Bolivian Air Force crashed in the populous city of Alto, barely missing a residential block.

More than 20 people died and another 30 were injured, and banknotes from the plane's cargo scattered around the city, prompting clashes between residents and security forces.
sentence: US defence company Lockheed Martin did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
context: US defence company Lockheed Martin did NIRTA request for comment .
